_spec.purpose: Automation-change trail. Phase 12 (AUTOMATION RETRO + UPGRADE) appends one entry per machine upgrade made after a run, both reactive fixes and frontier improvements: engine scripts, helpers, prompts, agent definitions. gmail_draft.py renders this run's entries into every dated Gmail draft so the maintainer can monitor the machine's evolution daily and pinpoint which week introduced a regression. Upgrades are committed separately (message prefix 'upgrade(<date>):') so a revert of that one commit rolls the change back cleanly.
rules: Never weaken a gate, threshold, or hard-fail rule; upgrades tighten, repair, or automate.
Every engine/script change is verified before commit (this run's slides + examples/demo-deck through render+qa, plus a defect-reconstruction test for new gates).
0-3 upgrades per run; zero is acceptable and is recorded as nothing (the email section then reads 'no changes').
Frontier improvements never displace a needed fix: the 0-3 budget fills reactive-first.

entry_schema.kind: fix (from this run's deviations) | improvement (from the frontier scan)
entry_schema.area: engine | scripts | assets | prompts | agents | knowledge
entry_schema.change: one sentence, what the machine now does differently
entry_schema.trigger: the run deviation that motivated it, or the source URL for a frontier improvement
entry_schema.verification: what was tested and the result
entry_schema.rollback: how to undo (usually: revert the named commit)

scan_log_schema.focus: which rotation area was scanned (must differ from the last 3 runs)
scan_log_schema.found: one-line summary of the most relevant findings

entries.change: qa.py now detects text-on-text overprint: per-line box intersection across distinct elements, FAIL for primary text pairs, WARN when decorative or data-overlap-ok is involved; render.py extracts per-line rects, DOM ancestry, and the new data-overlap-ok intent attribute, and data-decorative/data-overlap-ok now inherit from ancestor containers.
entries.trigger: Run 1 shipped-then-caught hard fail: slide-3 body copy rendered over the 'URBAN ALASKA GRID PEAK' bar label; machine QA passed it because each element sat in its own safe zone, and only the scorer's transcription caught it (the pixel-critic subagents were session-limit-blocked).
entries.verification: Reconstructed pre-fix slide-3 FAILS with 'text collision (100% overprint)'; the 9 shipped slides pass with 0 fails (cover chip demoted to WARN via data-overlap-ok); examples/demo-deck passes clean.

entries.change: bootstrap.sh probes that pypdf actually IMPORTS (in a subprocess) rather than merely exists, and repairs the Debian cryptography-41 pyo3 panic by upgrading cryptography; dependency probe also catches non-ImportError import crashes.
entries.trigger: Run 1: assemble.py's vector-PDF path crashed mid-run on 'pyo3_runtime.PanicException' from the system cryptography package; recovered only by a manual pip upgrade.
entries.verification: bootstrap.sh runs clean end-to-end on this container and reports 'pypdf import: ok'; assemble.py produced the run's vector PDF after the same fix applied manually.

entries.change: New committed helper assets/js/akgeo.js: AKGeo.alaskaProjection (canonical fit) and AKGeo.zoomTo (baseline full-state fit, then scale-and-retranslate regional zoom) so zoomed maps stop re-discovering the fitExtent-to-small-bbox trap; documented in SKILL.md with the stroke-only-at-zoom rule.
entries.trigger: Run 1: slides 2 and 7 both rendered a giant fill disc + mis-scaled map from fitExtent on a small lon/lat bbox and had to be rebuilt mid-run with the manual recipe.
entries.verification: Recipe is the exact code that fixed slides 2 and 7 in the shipped deck (both pass QA and were visually verified at full size).

entries.change: New Phase 12 AUTOMATION RETRO + UPGRADE in the master routine (analyze run vs spec, implement 0-3 verified bounded upgrades, log here, commit separately as 'upgrade(<date>):'), and gmail_draft.py now renders an 'Automation changes this run' section from this ledger into every dated draft.
entries.trigger: Maintainer request after run 1: make the automation self-improving with a daily-monitorable, dated, rollback-able trail of what changed.
entries.verification: gmail_draft.py re-run against the shipped run 1 artifacts renders the new section with these entries; payload builds clean.

entries.change: New committed helper assets/js/aktype.js: AK.fitText(el, {min, max, maxLines}) binary-searches a display headline's font-size until it renders in maxLines line boxes with no overflow (clamps to min + sets data-fit-overflow=1 if the box is genuinely too small), so a large headline can never silently soft-wrap an extra line into the block below it; wired into DESIGN_DOCTRINE section 2 and SKILL.md as the one fit-to-box implementation for future decks.
entries.trigger: Run 2026-07-09 recurring #1 defect: slides 1, 4, 7, 8 each set a large Archivo headline in a container narrower than its longest line; each soft-wrapped an extra line and machine_qa FAILED it as a text-overprint, all four repaired by hand. The doctrine prescribed a binary-search fit-to-box recipe but there was no shared helper, so every deck re-implemented or forgot it.
entries.verification: Defect reconstruction (80px headline in a 560px box, no helper) FAILS qa.py with 'text collision (53% overprint)' at 5 line boxes; identical markup WITH AK.fitText({min:40,max:80,maxLines:3}) converges to 58.5px / 3 line boxes and PASSES qa.py. This run's slides (out/2026-07-09/slides) render OK, qa WARN, 0 fails (unchanged from baseline); examples/demo-deck renders OK, qa PASS (unchanged). No gate weakened; no new runtime dependency (vanilla offline JS); shipped slides not modified.
entries.commit: upgrade(2026-07-09) commit on branch tsturg/festive-darwin-c9jczw (revert this single prefixed commit to roll back)

entries.change: qa.py now emits a WARN-level 'busy art under text' tripwire: for each PRIMARY text line box (font_px>=30) it samples the rendered PNG, masks the glyph ink (plus a 2px dilation), and warns when the remaining background carries high-contrast structured edges (bg edge density >= 0.03) -- i.e. a canvas/bitmap arc or texture crossing text that the DOM-only collision gate cannot see. WARN only; it points the pixel critics at a box and never FAILs.
entries.trigger: Run 2026-07-10 clearest gate blind spot: machine_qa PASSED all 9 slides (0/0) yet a canvas flightpath arc crossed two S3 body lines and a canvas orbit arc crossed the S4 headline; only the pixel critics caught them, because text_collisions() compares DOM/SVG text against DOM/SVG text and canvas ink is a bitmap invisible to render.py's DOM walk.
entries.verification: Reconstruction (body copy over a bright canvas arc + dashed grid) WARNs at bg edge density 0.04 while its clean control (same arc routed above the text) stays silent -> the tripwire discriminates the defect. This run's 9 shipped slides stay PASS 0 fails 0 warns (no false positives on the real, well-composed deck). examples/demo-deck still exits 0 (PASS -> WARN via one sensible warn on slide-03's display line over the generative structure field; no new FAILs). Clean-slide noise floor measured at <=0.010 vs threshold 0.03. No gate weakened (WARN only), no new runtime dependency (numpy/PIL already required by qa.py).

entries.change: New scripts/style_lint.py: a pre-flight CLI that applies site_build.py's exact prose-colon rule (byte-for-byte replica: strip script/style, tags->newlines, drop URLs and clock times, then any remaining ':' on a line is a violation) to arbitrary text via stdin, --file, or --file X.json --json-field FIELD (dot-path; '.' lints every string). Exits 1 with the offending line. Phase 3.5 and Phase 6 of the routine now lint the docket note and copy.json emitted fields with it BEFORE ship.
entries.trigger: Run 2026-07-10 repeat friction: site_build's prose-colon ship gate fired TWICE in Phase 11 (a docket history note, then copy.json first_comment 'Sources for today's deck:'), forcing manual rephrase at ship; run 2026-07-09 hit the same class twice. FIELD_NOTES 2026-07-09 parked a pre-flight colon lint. The ship gate is correct and stays unchanged; this only moves the catch earlier.
entries.verification: style_lint FAILs (exit 1) on both of this run's original ship-time failing strings (the first_comment colon line and a '- Update:' docket-note line); PASSes the fixed copy.json first_comment field; exempts clock times (4:30) and URLs exactly as site_build does; a direct cross-check confirms colon_violations() matches site_build.prose_colon_gate on clock/URL/ratio cases. No gate touched or weakened; no new dependency.

entries.change: New committed helper assets/js/aklabel.js: AK.canvasLabel(cx,x,y,text,{color,align}) draws any canvas-rendered label with an opaque knockout plate under the glyphs (auto-picking a near-black/near-white plate from the text luminance), so an in-scene label's contrast depends on (text,plate) only and never on the art beneath; AK.rectsOverlap keeps stacked labels from merging. Documented in SKILL.md. Opt-in; DOM/SVG text still preferred where layout allows.
entries.trigger: Run 2026-07-11 dominant defect class: machine_qa PASSED 0/0 yet the pixel critics caught ~10 canvas/decorative labels that were illegible -- S4 mono readouts ~1.5:1 on the ochre strata band, S6 SUSPENDED tag, S7 two flag labels overprinting into 'NSTARCORP', S8 context line on ochre. Canvas-drawn text is a bitmap with no DOM node, so text_collisions(), contrast_estimate(), and the 2026-07-10 busy-art tripwire (all of which walk render.py's DOM text_nodes) are structurally blind to it; the deck was repaired by hand with inline knockout chips. This commits that hand-fix as reusable machinery. (A canvas-text-aware QA gate was assessed and PARKED: not safely boundable without OCR/false-positives on dense sectional artwork -- FIELD_NOTES 2026-07-11.)
entries.verification: Reconstruction over an fbm-mottled ochre band (#B0803A) with the exact failing text/colours: RAW cx.fillText label reads ~1.9:1 (reproduces the shipped-then-caught defect) and RAW ENSTAR/HILCORP merge into 'ENSTAHILCORP' at ~2.8:1; the SAME labels via AK.canvasLabel read ~8.5:1 (bg = plate rgb(26,25,23), independent of the ochre) and ~12.9:1, and AK.rectsOverlap keeps them apart -- confirmed visually and by a pixel sampler using qa.py's contrast method. Regression: out/2026-07-11/slides render OK + qa PASS 0/0 and examples/demo-deck render OK + qa WARN (the single known 2026-07-10 slide-03 busy-art warn), both unchanged (new asset is unreferenced). qa.py on the verification slide: PASS 0/0 (no false positives). No gate/threshold weakened; no new runtime dependency (vanilla offline JS); shipped slides not modified.
entries.commit: upgrade(2026-07-11) commit 814b8c4 on PR #22 (revert this single prefixed commit to roll back)

scan_log.focus: self-improving-pipeline patterns (bootstrap week: the loop itself was built)
scan_log.found: Maintainer-directed: dated-email upgrade trail + frontier scan design adopted

scan_log.focus: typography craft / headless text-fitting (fit-to-box, text-wrap balance/pretty, variable-font width-axis fitting)
scan_log.found: Binary-search fit-to-box is the settled technique (~8 probes, sub-ms); CSS text-wrap: balance/pretty explicitly does NOT guarantee line count or prevent overflow (Chrome docs), so the wrap-collision fix must be JS. Variable-font wdth-axis fitting (fit-to-width.js) is a promising portable refinement.
scan_log.outcome: applied (aktype.js AK.fitText fix) + parked (wdth-axis fitting; colon pre-flight lint) FIELD_NOTES 2026-07-09

scan_log.focus: editorial dataviz / cartography technique (best news-graphics desks; rotated off self-improving-pipeline 07-08 and typography 07-09)
scan_log.found: Bloomberg concentric-radial-rings seasonal small multiple (calendar wrapped to a ring, each ring a variable; portable to offline seeded SVG/Canvas). Data-journalist map design space (arxiv 2508.10903, 462 maps / 5 outlets / 8 dimensions) as a treatment-director reference. Cartographic-narrative roundups reconfirmed the static subset we already practice; nothing new to change in the machine.
scan_log.outcome: parked (FIELD_NOTES 2026-07-10: radial-rings technique candidate + map design-space reference); no frontier code applied (the two reactive fixes filled the budget)

scan_log.focus: generative/procedural art portable to offline Canvas/SVG (texture/relief/noise) -- rotated off self-improving-pipeline 07-08, typography 07-09, editorial-dataviz/cartography 07-10; chosen to serve this run's weakest rubric (artwork craft = 7: flat strata bands, plain lenses)
scan_log.found: Inigo Quilez fbm article: additive noise octaves with gain=2^-H; H=1 (gain 0.5), ~5-6 octaves matches real mountain/rock spectra (-9dB/octave) -- the geological parameter set. Domain-warp article: f(g(p)) marbling. KEY: these primitives are ALREADY committed in noise.js (AK.fbm2/fbm3/warp2/simplex2), so a strata-texture/rim-light craft helper is a thin painterly layer over existing math, not new deps; relief needs only a lighter upper-left rim stroke on filled shapes plus low-alpha clipped fbm mottling.
scan_log.outcome: parked (FIELD_NOTES 2026-07-11: strata-texture / rim-light craft helper with concrete parameters + source URLs); no frontier code applied this run -- one reactive fix (aklabel.js) took the slot and the craft helper is held to the next improvement slot the day after a 4-upgrade day
